definition: The quality factor of an audio filter is the ratio of the height of the center frequency of the filter to the bandwidth of the filter at the 3dB point.

Any method of managing network data traffic to preserve system functionality and provide the required bandwidth for critical applications. Typically, QoS involves some combination of bandwidth allocation and data prioritization.
A point on a ground system that does not inject spurious voltages into the electronic system. There are no standards to measure how quiet a quiet ground is.
